earl-grey-hot-toddy.name: Earl Grey Hot Toddy
instructions: Bring water to boil in a pot, then add the tea bags. Steep at a low temperature for 5 minutes.
Add the lemon juice, honey, and whiskey and simmer for 5 more minutes.
Enjoy!

easter-cod-au-gratin-food-processor-tele.name: Easter Cod Au Gratin - Food Processor
easter-cod-au-gratin-food-processor-tele.original_name: Bacalhau gratinado de Páscoa - robot de cozinha
instructions: Light a frying pan with oil and let it warm. Pass the cod posts by flour, take them to fry on both sides, remove and reserve.

Peel the onions and garlic, cut the onions into half moons and pinch the garlic teeth. Praise them in a frying pan with olive oil, add the finely chopped naked tomato and let it saute until soft. Correct the seasonings, remove from the fire and reserve.
Peel the potatoes and cook them in salted water for 35 minutes. Spill the potatoes, pass them through the pass, add the milk and butter, season with nutmeg and stir well.

Lay the cod posts on a platter and lay the onion on top. Place the mashed mashed potatoes in a pastry bag with frizzy mouth and dispose around the cod. Finally, put the mayonnaise on the cod posts and serve.

easter-cupcakes-teleculinaria.name: Easter Cupcakes
instructions: Garnish small shapes with butter and sprinkle them with flour. Start the oven at 180 degrees.
In a bowl, hit the butter well with the sugar. Then add the eggs, one by one and always hitting very well between each addition. Then add the yeast, flour and cocoa and tap it again very well.
Distribute the dough through the shapes and put it in the oven for 20 minutes, or until it is cooked. Remove it from the oven, let it cool down and peel off.
Prepare the cream: Lay the ingredients indicated in a bowl and beat them well. Put the cream in a pastry bag and decorate the top of the cupcakes. Embellish with Easter almonds and serve.

ebi-no-chiri-sosu-japanese-shrimp-in-chi.name: Ebi No Chiri-sōsu (japanese Shrimp In Chile Sauce)

ingredients.item: (3/4 ounce; 20g) plus 1 teaspoon unmodified potato starch

ingredients.prep: plus more if needed; for table salt, use half as much by volume
such as Chinese doubanjiang or Japanese tobanjan (see note)

ingredients.item: Homemade or store-bought chicken stock or water
Negi (welsh onion) or 1/2 small leek (2 ounces; 55g)
ingredients.prep: white part only, rinsed well and minced (see note)

instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together 1 teaspoon potato starch with 1/4 cup (60ml) cold water until well combined; set aside.

Rinse the shrimp under cold running water, massaging the shrimp with your hands, for 1 minute. In a medium bowl, combine the rinsed shrimp, baking soda, egg white, and salt. Add remaining 2 tablespoons potato starch and, using a spoon or clean hands, toss to coat shrimp.

Place a wire rack over a rimmed baking sheet. In a carbon-steel wok or 10-inch carbon steel or stainless-steel skillet, heat oil over high heat to 166°C (165°C). Add shrimp, discarding any excess marinade and dropping from as close to the oil’s surface as possible to minimize splashing, and fry, stirring and turning occasionally, until cooked through, about 2 minutes. (Potato starch will not brown significantly like wheat flour would, so the cooked shrimp will still appear quite pale.) Using a spider or slotted spoon, transfer shrimp to prepared baking sheet. Carefully ladle oil through a fine-mesh strainer set over a medium heatproof bowl.

Return 2 tablespoons (30ml) oil to now-empty wok and return the wok to high heat (allow remaining oil to fully cool before storing). Add ginger and garlic and cook, stirring constantly, until softened but not browned, about 10 seconds. Add chile-bean paste and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Stir in chicken stock, ketchup, sugar, vinegar, sake, and negi (or leek). Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer and cook, stirring, until negi (leek) is softened and tender, about 2 minutes. Adjust seasoning to taste with sugar, vinegar, or salt as required.

Add reserved shrimp and toss to coat. Stir in potato starch slurry, a teaspoon or two at a time, until sauce thickens enough to coat the shrimp in a glaze; discard any remaining potato starch-water mixture or reserve for another use. If sauce over-thickens and becomes gloppy, loosen by stirring in water 1 tablespoon at a time.
Transfer to a plate and serve immediately.

eggplant-and-onion-pastry-lezzet.name: Eggplant And Onion Pastry
instructions: 1. Shake the eggplants in small cubes.
2.  Tuzlu suda 15 dakika bekletin.
3. rinse and dry with paper towel.
4. Peel the onions and tomatoes and rub the small chop.
5. Take the liquid pan and tease it.
6. Add and fry the eggplants.
7. Add excess oil in the pan and add onions to the eggplant and squeeze it with stirring.
8. Add tomatoes and add salt and mix.
9. Leave cooking and spreading up to 10 minutes.
10. Oil the oven tray.
11. 11. One of the Yufka posts on the tray to wrap the edges out.
12. Take the Margarini on the soft brush.
12. August Divided the remaining yufka into large parts and immerse in melted oil and lay half into the tray.
14 July Close the eggplanty material on or on the remaining oily pieces.
15. Trim the stones from the edges.
16. Remove the egg on a pinch with salt and over the bör.
17. September Bake for 40 minutes in the oven with a pre-heated 200 degrees.
18. Serve slice and hot.

eggplant-and-tomato-caponata.name: Eggplant And Tomato Caponata
instructions: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat; saute eggplant, onion, and garlic until lightly browned, 5 to 7 minutes. Add tomatoes, red wine, and capers and simmer until heated through, about 5 minutes.

Mix oregano, cinnamon, allspice, cocoa powder, sugar, and bay leaf into eggplant mixture; simmer until thickened, 30 minutes. Add a few tablespoons water if mixture becomes too thick. Remove bay leaf.

el-paso-stuffed-potato.name: El Paso Stuffed Potato

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and ground black pepper

instructions: Prepare a smoker for cooking at 149°C. (An oven may be used if a smoker is not available.)
Rinse the potato thoroughly and wrap it in aluminum foil. Place the wrapped potato in the smoker and smoke for 3 hours. Roll the potato on the counter to soften it.

Open and remove the foil from the potato, then cut a slit down its center. Layer the potato with butter, salt, pepper, cheese, sour cream, chives, the prepared Beans, and Smoked Brisket, then top with a Jalapeno Toreado.
For the Beans: Sauté the bacon and onions in a skillet over medium heat until golden brown; set aside.

Place the dry pinto beans, chili powder, 2 tablespoons kosher salt, 2 tablespoons ground black pepper, and 2 1/2 quarts of water into a small pressure cooker. Add the cooked bacon and onions. Pressure cook according to the manufacturer's specifications for 2 hours.
For the Smoked Brisket: Preheat an offset smoker to 121°C.

Trim the brisket. Sprinkle it all over with kosher salt and ground black pepper. Cook in the smoker until the meat is "floppy" and has an internal temperature of 93°C all the way through, which will take 10 to 18 hours.
Wrap the brisket in butcher paper and continue cooking in the smoker until its internal temperature rises above 93°C, an additional 2 to 6 hours.
Remove the brisket from the smoker. Let it rest for 1 hour. Slice and chop the meat.
For the Jalapeno Toreado: Brown the raw jalapenos in a cast-iron skillet on medium-high heat until soft, about 10 minutes. Remove them to a bowl.
Add soy sauce, fresh lime juice, and the sliced raw white onion to a separate bowl. Toss the browned jalapenos in this marinade and refrigerate overnight.

enjoy-these-thai-style-pork-skewers-come.name: Enjoy These Thai-style Pork Skewers
enjoy-these-thai-style-pork-skewers-come.original_name: Disfruta de estas brochetas de cerdo al estilo tailandés

ingredients.item: 1 cdita de granos de pimienta blanca

instructions: Trocea el cilantro finamente.
Agrega en un mortero los dientes de ajo, el cilantro troceado, y la pimienta en granos. Tritura todos los ingredientes hasta que forme una pasta. Reserva.
Take the pork back and cut into small, macer cubes till they light up a little.
Put the pork in a bowl and add garlic, cilantro and pepper. Also pour fish sauce, oyster sauce, soya sauce, salt and sugar.
Mezcla muy bien hasta que el cerdo quede aderezado y marina en la nevera durante dos horas para que tome todo el sabor.
Once they are marinated, they heat up a grill or an iron.
Take the knipes and put the pork into them. I recommend you put only 6 to 7 pieces per pinch.
When the grill or plate are at temperature, place the knipes and cook for 7 minutes from each side or until they are doradite.
In the cooking process scattering with coconut milk so as to avoid losing juicy.
Retract from the grill as soon as they are cooked and taste.

enjoy-this-vegan-white-chocolate-cake-co.name: Enjoy This Vegan White Chocolate Cake
enjoy-this-vegan-white-chocolate-cake-co.original_name: Disfruta de este bizcocho vegano de chocolate blanco
instructions: Let's start our recipe by heating the oven at 180 ° C
En un bol, tamizamos con un colador la harina y el bicarbonato de sodio para eliminar los grumos.

Now, in a bowl mix the milk of almonds and the vanilla. We will open up a space in the middle of the flour mix, a volcano type, and proceeds to add little by little the liquid mixture and vegetable oil.
Drop the vegan white chocolate to a pawnout.

Bat with a manual beaker till you have a pasty texture. It's up to you to add the vegan white chocolate. With a spatula you put them together very well till you have a homogenous and very achocolated mixture.
Take the mold where you'll bake the biscuit and grease and make it worse.
Fold the mixture into the mold and once built up give them a few jobs till everything sits.
Lleva tu bizcocho al horno de 25 a 30 minutos aproximadamente para que se cocine.
At 25 minutes, apply paddle technique. Enter one, if it gets wet it's missing more cooking and you have to leave it for longer. Otherwise get him out of the oven.
Let them cool at the mold for 10 minutes and then move them to the grid. Let them cool for an hour.
Cut them into triangles or slices and enjoy them.

everything-breakfast-pretzels.name: Everything Breakfast Pretzels

ingredients.prep: cut into 4 pieces, at room temperature

instructions: For the tangzhong: In a small saucepan, combine the water and 2 tablespoons of flour. Heat over low heat, whisking constantly, until the mixture is shiny and thick, 2 to 3 minutes. Scrape into a small bowl and set aside to cool slightly.

For the dough: In the same pan, combine the milk, granulated sugar, and salt. Heat, stirring constantly, until just simmering and the sugar and salt have dissolved, 2 to 3 minutes. Set aside to cool until the milk registers 100 to 43°C on an instant-read thermometer.

Grease a large bowl and set aside. In the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with a dough hook, combine the warm milk and the yeast and let sit until foamy, 5 minutes. Add the slightly cooled tangzhong and egg and stir to combine.

With the mixer on low speed, add the flour and mix until incorporated and the dough is smooth and has formed into a cohesive ball, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the butter, one piece at a time, mixing until incorporated and scraping down the sides of the bowl and the hook as needed. Increase the speed to medium and knead the dough until it is sticky but elastic. It should be pulling away from the sides of the bowl and starting to gather around the hook while still pooling on the bottom of the bowl, 7 to 10 minutes. Gently pull a small section of the dough; you should be able to count to 5 without it tearing off. Lightly flour your hands and pull the ends of the dough to form a smooth ball. Transfer the dough to the large greased bowl and let the dough rise until doubled in size, 1½ to 2 hours. The dough can be used immediately or refrigerated for up to 2 days.
Line a sheet pan with parchment paper and grease the paper lightly with baking spray.

Generously flour your work surface. Divide the dough into six equal pieces, weighing about 4 oz (113 g) each. Roll a piece back and forth with your hands to form a long rope about 24 in (60 cm) long, flouring lightly as needed. As you make the rope, leave the center slightly fatter and gradually taper the ends.

Twist the rope into a pretzel shape: Bring the ends of the rope away from you and form a U or horseshoe shape. Bend the ends to bring them toward the bottom of the U, crossing them over each other and then twisting them, if desired. Rest the ends on top of the base of the pretzel or tuck them underneath. Transfer the shaped pretzels to the prepared sheet pan, leaving about 3 in of space in between them, and cover loosely with plastic wrap. Repeat with the remaining pieces of dough, shaping them into pretzels and covering them. Let the pretzels rise for 45 minutes.
Position an oven rack in the middle of the oven and preheat the oven to 232°C. Line two more sheet pans, one with parchment paper lightly dusted with flour and another with just parchment paper.

In a large stockpot or Dutch oven, bring the water to a boil over high heat. Lower the heat so the water is simmering, then add the baking soda and granulated sugar to the water. Drop one pretzel into the water; it should float. Simmer for about 1 minute, then flip it over and simmer for another 30 to 45 seconds. Transfer to the flour-dusted sheet pan. When it has cooled for 1 or 2 minutes, transfer to the parchment-lined sheet pan. Repeat with the remaining five pretzels.

Put ¼ cup (60 g) of the simmering cooking water in a small heatproof bowl or measuring cup and use a pastry brush to brush the tops of the pretzels lightly with the water. Sprinkle with the bagel seasoning and salt.
Bake until the pretzels are golden brown and firm, 12 to 15 minutes. Transfer the sheet pan to a wire rack and let cool until the pretzels are just warm.
Pretzels are best eaten when they have firmed up a bit but are still warm to the touch, but they can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days.

everything-spice-fish-with-tomato-salad.name: Everything-spice Fish With Tomato Salad
instructions: Place 1 cup plain whole-milk Greek yogurt in a small bowl and finely grate in zest from 1 lemon. Season with kosher salt and mix well. Cut lemon in half; set aside. Chill lemony yogurt until ready to serve.

Place ½ cup all-purpose flour in a shallow bowl. Lightly beat 2 large eggs in another shallow bowl to blend. Mix 1¼ cups panko, ½ cup raw white sesame seeds, and ⅓ cup everything bagel seasoning in a third shallow bowl.

Pat 4 thin Dover sole fillets (about 1½ lb.) dry and season with salt. Working one at a time, dredge fillets in flour, shaking off excess, then dip in egg, letting excess drip back into bowl. Dredge in panko mixture, pressing to adhere. Transfer fish to a baking sheet.
Pour vegetable oil into a large skillet to come ¼" up sides and heat over medium-high. Working in batches, cook fish until golden brown and crisp, 1–2 minutes per side. Transfer to a wire rack.

Cut 1 English hothouse cucumber in half lengthwise, then thinly slice crosswise. Combine cucumber, ½ medium red onion, thinly sliced, 2 cups halved cherry tomatoes, 3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, and 2 Tbsp. chopped drained capers in a large bowl. Squeeze in juice from reserved lemon halves and season with salt; toss to combine. Add ½ cup coarsely chopped dill; gently toss again. Taste and season salad with more salt if needed.
Spread some lemony yogurt on one side of each plate. Place fish alongside and top with salad; season with freshly ground black pepper.

extra-large-latkes.name: Extra-large Latkes

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or 1 0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt
. melted schmaltz (chicken fat) or vegetable oil

instructions: Grate 1¼ lb. russet potatoes (2–3 medium), scrubbed, on the large holes of a box grater; place in a large bowl lined with a kitchen towel. Add 1 large onion, very thinly sliced, and mix well, breaking apart onion rings. Wrap towel tightly around mixture and squeeze over bowl to release as much liquid as you can. Discard liquid; reserve bowl (do not rinse out; you’ll want that bit of potato starch later). Set potato mixture in towel aside.

Whisk 1 large egg, beaten to blend, ¼ cup all-purpose flour, 2½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1½ tsp. Morton kosher salt, and ½ tsp. fresh ground pepper in reserved bowl until a thick slurry forms. Add reserved potato mixture to bowl and toss with your hands until slurry is completely mixed into potato mixture. Let sit 15 minutes.

Heat 1 Tbsp. melted schmaltz (chicken fat) or vegetable oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium. Swirl fat to evenly coat pan, then add half of potato mixture to the center of the skillet with tongs. Gently press out to make a 9"-diameter round, leaving a 1½" border around edges of skillet; press stray pieces and craggy edges down with a spatula so they make direct contact with pan. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook latke until deep golden brown underneath, 6–8 minutes. Using a heatproof rubber spatula, loosen latke so it slides around when pan is shaken. Gently slide latke onto a large plate (browned side should be down). Drizzle any fat in pan over latke. Using dry kitchen towels or oven mitts, place skillet upside down over plate and quickly flip over pan and plate in a single motion to return latke to skillet; remove plate. Place skillet back over medium-low heat; pour 1 Tbsp. melted schmaltz (chicken fat) or vegetable oil around latke, lifting so fat runs underneath and pulling out any craggy edges that may have gotten tucked under. Cook until deep golden brown on second side and cooked through, 6–8 minutes. Slide latke out of pan onto a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet. Repeat process with remaining potato mixture and 2 Tbsp. melted schmaltz (chicken fat) or vegetable oil to make another latke.

Transfer each latke to a large plate and mound 2 Tbsp. mixed tender herbs (such as dill, parsley, and/or chives) coarsely chopped, on top of each one. Serve with sour cream and/or applesauce for spooning over if desired.
Do ahead: Latkes can be made 1 day ahead. Let cool completely, then wrap tightly, leaving on rack in baking sheet, and chill. Just before serving, unwrap and reheat (still on rack) in a 350° oven until warmed through, about 5 minutes.
